d: Core tools for programmatic Figma work: figma_execute (arbitrary JS with full figma API access — most powerful), figma_create_child (add shapes/frames/text inside parent), figma_set_fills/figma_set_strokes (colors), figma_set_text (text content), figma_resize_node/figma_move_node (dimensions/position), figma_clone_node (duplicate), figma_delete_node (remove), figma_rename_node (rename layer). Component tools: figma_add_component_property, figma_edit_component_property, figma_delete_component_property, figma_instantiate_component, figma_set_instance_properties, figma_arrange_component_set. Variable tools: figma_create_variable_collection, figma_create_variable, figma_batch_create_variables, figma_update_variable, figma_batch_update_variables, figma_setup_design_tokens, figma_add_mode. Visual validation: figma_capture_screenshot, figma_take_screenshot.

figma_execute runs arbitrary JavaScript in Figma's plugin context. Has full access to the figma global object. Use for: creating components (figma.createComponent()), combining variants (figma.combineAsVariants()), traversing document tree, batch operations, anything the dedicated tools cant do. RULES: 30s timeout max. Always return results as JSON. Slice large result arrays to prevent timeout. For component instances (node.type=INSTANCE), use figma_set_instance_properties instead of direct text editing — direct editing FAILS SILENTLY. After creating/modifying visuals, validate with figma_capture_screenshot.

Decision tree for choosing the right tool: CREATE a shape/frame/text → figma_create_child. CREATE a component from scratch → figma_execute (createComponent). MODIFY fills/strokes → figma_set_fills/figma_set_strokes. MODIFY text content → figma_set_text (for regular text) or figma_set_instance_properties (for component instances). ADD component properties → figma_add_component_property. CREATE variables → figma_create_variable or figma_batch_create_variables. FULL TOKEN SETUP → figma_setup_design_tokens. COMPLEX multi-step operations → figma_execute. VALIDATE results → figma_capture_screenshot. RULE: Prefer dedicated tools over figma_execute when available — they have better error handling and validation.

Step-by-step to create a button component: 1) figma_execute: const frame = figma.createFrame(); frame.name = 'Button'; frame.layoutMode = 'HORIZONTAL'; frame.primaryAxisAlignItems = 'CENTER'; frame.counterAxisAlignItems = 'CENTER'; frame.paddingLeft = frame.paddingRight = 24; frame.paddingTop = frame.paddingBottom = 12; frame.cornerRadius = 8; frame.fills = [{type:'SOLID',color:{r:0.23,g:0.47,b:0.93}}]; frame.itemSpacing = 8; const text = figma.createText(); await figma.loadFontAsync({family:'Inter',style:'Medium'}); text.fontName = {family:'Inter',style:'Medium'}; text.characters = 'Button'; text.fontSize = 16; text.fills = [{type:'SOLID',color:{r:1,g:1,b:1}}]; frame.appendChild(text); const comp = figma.createComponentFromNode(frame); return {id: comp.id}; 2) figma_add_component_property: name='Label', type=TEXT, defaultValue='Button'. 3) figma_capture_screenshot to verify.

t: Recipe: Create Button with Icon + Variants
d: Full button with icon toggle and size variants: 1) Create base button (see ex-004). 2) Add icon frame inside: figma_create_child(parentId, FRAME, {name:'Icon',width:20,height:20}). 3) figma_add_component_property: 'Show Icon' as BOOLEAN on icon layer. 4) Clone component 2x with figma_clone_node for size variants. 5) Resize clones: figma_resize_node for Medium(padding 12/24) and Small(padding 8/16, fontSize 14). 6) Rename variants: 'Size=Large', 'Size=Medium', 'Size=Small'. 7) figma_execute: const variants = [node1, node2, node3]; figma.combineAsVariants(variants, figma.currentPage); 8) figma_arrange_component_set to organize grid. 9) Validate with figma_capture_screenshot.

Add interactive states to component: 1) Start with base component. 2) Clone for each state: Default, Hover, Active, Disabled, Focus. 3) Modify each clone: Hover → slightly darker fill (use figma_set_fills). Active → even darker, slight scale. Disabled → opacity 0.4, no pointer events. Focus → add 2px outline offset ring (figma_set_strokes or add outer frame with border). 4) Rename each: 'State=Default', 'State=Hover', 'State=Active', 'State=Disabled', 'State=Focus'. 5) Combine as variants with figma_execute: figma.combineAsVariants(). 6) In prototype: set Change To interactions between state variants on hover/press triggers. 7) figma_arrange_component_set. 8) Validate all states with figma_capture_screenshot.

Text input component: 1) figma_execute: Create outer frame (vertical AL, gap 4). Create label text ('Label', 14px, medium, gray-700). Create input frame (horizontal AL, h:48, padding 12/16, border 1px gray-300, radius 8, fill white). Create placeholder text inside input ('Placeholder', 16px, regular, gray-400). Create helper text below ('Helper text', 12px, regular, gray-500). Convert to component. 2) Add properties: 'Label' TEXT, 'Placeholder' TEXT, 'Helper Text' TEXT, 'Show Helper' BOOLEAN. 3) Create variants for states: Default, Focus(border blue-500, ring), Error(border red-500, helper red), Disabled(bg gray-100, opacity 0.6). 4) Combine as variants. 5) figma_arrange_component_set.

Card compound component: 1) Create sub-components first: CardImage (frame, fill image, fixed height 200, fill width), CardBody (vertical AL, padding 16, gap 8, fill width), CardFooter (horizontal AL, padding 16, gap 8, space-between). 2) Create Card main frame: vertical AL, width 320, radius 12, fill white, shadow(0 2 8 rgba(0,0,0,0.1)), clip content true. 3) Add children: CardImage instance + CardBody + CardFooter. 4) In CardBody: Title text (18px semibold) + Description text (14px regular gray-600). 5) In CardFooter: Button instance (instance swap) + secondary action. 6) Convert to component. Properties: 'Title' TEXT, 'Description' TEXT, 'Show Image' BOOLEAN, 'Show Footer' BOOLEAN. 7) Validate layout at different widths.

Top navigation bar: 1) Create frame: horizontal AL, width fill-container, height 64, padding 0/16, items center, fill white, shadow(0 1 3 rgba(0,0,0,0.1)). 2) Left section: frame horizontal AL, gap 8, hug. Add logo placeholder (32x32 frame). 3) Center section: frame horizontal AL, gap 24, fill container, justify center. Add nav links as text ('Home','Products','About','Contact'). 4) Right section: frame horizontal AL, gap 12, hug. Add CTA button instance + avatar (32x32 circle). 5) Convert to component. Properties: 'Logo' INSTANCE_SWAP, 'Show CTA' BOOLEAN, nav link texts. 6) Create Mobile variant: hamburger icon replacing center nav, right section hidden. 7) Combine as variants: 'Breakpoint=Desktop', 'Breakpoint=Mobile'.

Icon button with size variants: 1) figma_execute: frame 40x40, auto layout center/center, radius 8, fill transparent. Add icon frame 24x24 centered inside. 2) Component properties: 'Icon' INSTANCE_SWAP (for swapping different icons). 3) Variants: Size=Large(48x48, icon 24), Size=Medium(40x40, icon 20), Size=Small(32x32, icon 16). Style variants: Style=Filled(bg primary, icon white), Style=Outlined(border 1px, bg transparent, icon primary), Style=Ghost(no border, no bg, icon primary). 4) State variants: Default, Hover, Active, Disabled. 5) Total variants: 3 sizes x 3 styles x 4 states = 36. OPTIMIZATION: Use variables for colors instead of style variants → reduces to 3x4=12 variants + variable modes.

t: Recipe: Create Full Color Token System
d: Complete color system with light/dark: 1) figma_create_variable_collection: 'Primitives' (single mode). 2) figma_batch_create_variables in Primitives: gray-50(#F9FAFB) through gray-950(#030712), blue-50(#EFF6FF) through blue-950(#172554), red-50 through red-950, green-50 through green-950, amber-50 through amber-950. ~50 color variables. 3) figma_create_variable_collection: 'Semantic' with modes Light, Dark. 4) figma_batch_create_variables in Semantic with aliases: text-primary → gray-900(light)/gray-50(dark), text-secondary → gray-600(light)/gray-400(dark), surface-primary → white(light)/gray-900(dark), surface-secondary → gray-50(light)/gray-800(dark), action-primary → blue-600(light)/blue-400(dark), border-default → gray-200(light)/gray-700(dark), error → red-600(light)/red-400(dark), success → green-600(light)/green-400(dark). 5) Apply to designs, switch modes to verify.

Spacing variable system: 1) figma_create_variable_collection: 'Spacing'. 2) figma_batch_create_variables (type NUMBER): spacing/0(0), spacing/0.5(2), spacing/1(4), spacing/1.5(6), spacing/2(8), spacing/2.5(10), spacing/3(12), spacing/4(16), spacing/5(20), spacing/6(24), spacing/8(32), spacing/10(40), spacing/12(48), spacing/16(64), spacing/20(80), spacing/24(96). 3) Scope all to: gap, padding, width, height. 4) Optional responsive modes: add Mobile/Desktop modes where spacing/4 = 12(mobile)/16(desktop) for tighter mobile layouts.

Border radius system: figma_batch_create_variables (type NUMBER): radius/none(0), radius/xs(2), radius/sm(4), radius/md(8), radius/lg(12), radius/xl(16), radius/2xl(20), radius/3xl(24), radius/full(9999). Scope to: corner-radius only. Apply: buttons use radius/md(8) or radius/lg(12), cards use radius/lg(12) or radius/xl(16), pills/badges use radius/full(9999), inputs use radius/md(8), modals use radius/xl(16) or radius/2xl(20).

Typography style creation (styles not variables — typography needs composite values): Use figma_execute to create text styles programmatically: const style = figma.createTextStyle(); style.name = 'Typography/Display/Large'; style.fontName = {family:'Inter',style:'Bold'}; style.fontSize = 48; style.lineHeight = {value:56, unit:'PIXELS'}; style.letterSpacing = {value:-0.5, unit:'PIXELS'}; Repeat for full scale: Display-L(48/56/-0.5), Display-M(36/44/-0.25), Display-S(30/36/0), Heading-1(28/36/-0.25), Heading-2(24/32/0), Heading-3(20/28/0), Body-L(18/28/0), Body-M(16/24/0), Body-S(14/20/0), Caption(12/16/0), Overline(11/16/0.5). RULE: Use Inter or SF Pro for iOS, Roboto for Android, Inter for cross-platform.

t: Recipe: Setup Design Tokens in One Shot
d: Use figma_setup_design_tokens for complete token structure in a single call. Example for brand colors: collectionName='Brand Colors', modes=['Light','Dark'], tokens=[{name:'color/primary',resolvedType:'COLOR',values:{Light:'#2563EB',Dark:'#60A5FA'}}, {name:'color/on-primary',resolvedType:'COLOR',values:{Light:'#FFFFFF',Dark:'#1E3A5F'}}, {name:'color/surface',resolvedType:'COLOR',values:{Light:'#FFFFFF',Dark:'#0F172A'}}, ...]. This creates collection + modes + all variables in one operation. FASTEST way to bootstrap a token system. Then create Semantic collection separately with aliases to these primitives.

Full DS creation sequence: PHASE 1 — TOKENS: 1) Create Primitives collection (colors: 50-950 per hue, spacing scale, radius scale, elevation). 2) Create Semantic collection with Light/Dark modes (text, surface, action, border, feedback tokens aliased to primitives). 3) Create typography text styles (display, heading, body, caption scale). 4) Create effect styles (shadow levels 1-5). 5) Create grid styles (mobile 4-col, tablet 8-col, desktop 12-col). PHASE 2 — ATOMS: 6) Build Button component (sizes, states, icon toggle). 7) Build Input component (states, helper text). 8) Build Badge/Tag, Checkbox, Radio, Switch, Avatar, Icon Button. PHASE 3 — MOLECULES: 9) Build Card, ListItem, FormField, Tooltip, Chip. PHASE 4 — ORGANISMS: 10) Build Navigation Bar, Sidebar, Modal, Bottom Sheet, Toast. PHASE 5 — VALIDATE: 11) Screenshot all components. 12) Test light/dark modes. 13) Run audit module checks.

Standard component documentation page in Figma: 1) Create section 'Component Name — All Variants'. Place component set with all variants arranged via figma_arrange_component_set. 2) Create section 'Usage Guidelines'. Add frames with Do/Dont examples side by side (green checkmark frame / red X frame). 3) Create section 'Specs'. Add annotated frame showing padding, spacing, sizes with redlines. 4) Create section 'Playground'. Add loose instances for testing overrides. Use figma_execute to create sections: const section = figma.createSection(); section.name = 'Button — All Variants'; then position component set inside.

Component that adapts to breakpoints: APPROACH A (variant-based): Create Desktop variant (horizontal layout, larger padding/text) and Mobile variant (vertical/stacked, smaller padding). Add Breakpoint variant property. Designers manually swap. APPROACH B (flexible auto-layout): Single component with wrap auto-layout. Children have min-width set. On narrow container, items stack. On wide, they flow horizontal. No variant swap needed. APPROACH C (responsive variables): Create 'Responsive' variable collection with Mobile/Desktop modes. Variables: content-padding(12/24), heading-size(20/28), gap(12/24). Bind to component. Switch mode on parent frame. RECOMMENDATION: Use B for simple adaptation, C for spacing/sizing changes, A only when layout fundamentally restructures.

Slot-based composable component (2025 feature): 1) Create Card component with fixed header and footer. 2) Add content area frame inside — mark as slot (figma_execute: node.isSlot = true or via UI). 3) Define slot constraints: which component types the slot accepts. 4) Designers using the instance can now drag ANY content into the slot area without detaching. 5) Slots replace the need for many content variants — one Card component handles all content types. USE CASES: Card body content, Modal body, List item content area, Toolbar action buttons, Form field groups. RULE: Always set slot constraints to prevent designers from inserting inappropriate content.

Data table with configurable columns: 1) Create TableHeader component: horizontal AL, fill width. Each column header = nested component with TEXT property and sort icon (BOOLEAN toggle). 2) Create TableRow component: horizontal AL, fill width, matching column widths. Each cell = nested component with TEXT property. 3) Create TableCell variants: Text, Number(right-aligned), Badge(status chip), Action(buttons), Checkbox(selection). 4) Create Table wrapper: vertical AL, fill width. Header + n rows. Use instance swap on cells for column types. 5) Properties: row count (manual), column widths (use percentage-based fill), alternating row colors (boolean), show header (boolean), show selection (boolean). COMPLEX — consider using figma_execute for bulk row creation.

Populate a page with component instances: figma_execute code: const comp = figma.root.findOne(n => n.type === 'COMPONENT' && n.name === 'Card'); const data = [{title:'Item 1',desc:'Description 1'}, {title:'Item 2',desc:'Description 2'}, ...]; const instances = []; for (let i = 0; i < data.length; i++) { const inst = comp.createInstance(); inst.x = (i % 3) * 340; inst.y = Math.floor(i / 3) * 420; instances.push(inst); } return {created: instances.length}; Then use figma_set_instance_properties on each to set text. RULE: Create instances in batches of 20-30 to avoid timeout. Use multiple figma_execute calls for large sets.

t: Recipe: Clone and Modify Existing Component
d: Extend an existing component: 1) figma_get_component(nodeId) to inspect the original. 2) figma_clone_node to duplicate it. 3) figma_rename_node to give new name. 4) Modify the clone: figma_set_fills for new colors, figma_resize_node for new dimensions, figma_set_text for new default text. 5) figma_add_component_property for new properties specific to this variant. 6) If both should be variants of same set: figma_execute with figma.combineAsVariants([original, clone], parent). RULE: Only clone to create genuinely different components — dont clone just to change colors (use variables instead).

Standard iOS screen setup: 1) Create frame 393x852 (iPhone 15/16). 2) Add status bar area: 59pt top (use absolute position or padding). 3) Add navigation bar frame: horizontal AL, h:44, padding 0/16, items center. Back chevron(left) + title(center, 17pt semibold) + action(right). 4) Add content area: vertical AL, fill container both axes, padding 16 horizontal. 5) Add tab bar at bottom: horizontal AL, h:49, 5 items evenly distributed. Each item: vertical AL center, icon 24x24 + label 10pt. 6) Add home indicator: 134x5 frame, centered, 8pt from bottom, inside 34pt safe area. 7) Apply iOS color variables: systemBackground for content, secondarySystemBackground for grouped sections. RULE: Use SF Pro for all text, SF Symbols for icons.

Standard Android M3 screen setup: 1) Create frame 412x915 (Pixel standard). 2) Add status bar: 24dp, transparent (content goes behind). 3) Add top app bar: horizontal AL, h:64, padding 0/16. Nav icon 48dp touch(24dp icon) + title(22sp title-large) + actions(max 3). 4) Add content area: vertical AL, fill container, padding 16 horizontal. 5) Add bottom navigation: horizontal AL, h:80, 3-5 items. Each item: vertical AL center, icon 24dp + label 12sp. Active: filled icon + primary color. Inactive: outlined + on-surface-variant. 6) Add gesture nav bar: 48dp transparent at bottom. RULE: Use Roboto for text, Material Symbols for icons. Apply M3 color tokens from tonal palette.

Set up interactive component with state changes: 1) Create component set with state variants (Default, Hover, Pressed, Focused). 2) In prototype mode, add interactions: On Default variant → While Hovering → Change to Hover (Smart Animate 150ms ease-out). On Hover variant → Mouse Leave → Change to Default (Smart Animate 150ms). On Hover variant → On Click → Change to Pressed (Smart Animate 50ms). On Pressed variant → After Delay 100ms → Change to Default. On Default variant → On Focus → Change to Focused. 3) Test in prototype preview. RULE: Keep transition durations short — hover 100-200ms, press 50-100ms, focus instant. Use Smart Animate for smooth property transitions.

CRITICAL: When editing text inside component INSTANCES, NEVER use figma_set_text directly on the text node. It FAILS SILENTLY — appears to work but changes dont persist. ALWAYS use figma_set_instance_properties with the text property name. Example: figma_set_instance_properties(nodeId='instance-id', properties={'Label':'New Text'}). Check instance.componentProperties to find available property names — they may have #nodeId suffixes like 'Label#123:456'. The tool handles suffix matching automatically.

CRITICAL: Before setting text in figma_execute, MUST load fonts first: await figma.loadFontAsync({family:'Inter',style:'Regular'}). This is async — use await. If font is not loaded, text operations silently fail or throw. Common fonts to preload: Inter(Regular,Medium,SemiBold,Bold), Roboto(Regular,Medium,Bold), SF Pro Display/Text. Load ALL weights youll use before any text operations. TIP: Load fonts once at start of script, then do all text operations.

t: Gotcha: Color Format in figma_execute vs Tools
d: Color formats differ between tools: figma_set_fills accepts HEX strings ('#FF0000'). figma_execute requires Figma RGB objects: {r:1,g:0,b:0} where values are 0-1 floats, NOT 0-255. Conversion: hex to figma RGB → r = parseInt(hex.slice(1,3),16)/255. Alpha/opacity: set separately on the fill object (opacity:0.5) or on the node (node.opacity=0.5). RULE: When using figma_execute for fills, always convert hex to 0-1 floats. When using figma_set_fills, pass hex strings directly.

In Figma plugin API, last child = topmost layer (drawn on top). When creating backgrounds: use parent.insertChild(0, backgroundNode) to place at BOTTOM of stack. parent.appendChild(node) places at TOP. Common mistake: creating background after content pushes it on top, hiding content. For auto layout frames: child order = display order (first child at start of flow). Reorder with parent.insertChild(index, node).

figma_execute has 30s max timeout (default 5s). For large operations: 1) Set timeout:30000 in tool call. 2) Process in batches — dont traverse entire 10,000-node file in one call. 3) Return early with partial results + continuation token. 4) Use multiple sequential figma_execute calls instead of one massive script. 5) Always slice results arrays: return results.slice(0, 50) to prevent serialization timeout. RULE: If operation might be slow, split into discovery (find nodes) + execution (modify nodes) in separate calls.

Two ways to create components in figma_execute: 1) figma.createComponent() — creates empty component frame. Add children manually. Returns ComponentNode. 2) figma.createComponentFromNode(existingFrame) — converts existing frame into component, preserving all children and properties. Returns ComponentNode. PREFER method 2: build your layout as a regular frame first (easier to iterate), then convert to component at the end. Method 1 is for programmatic generation where you build structure in code.

After every creation/modification operation, validate: 1) figma_capture_screenshot(nodeId) — captures CURRENT plugin state (reliable for just-made changes). 2) Inspect the screenshot: check alignment, spacing, proportions, text rendering. 3) If issues found: adjust with figma_move_node, figma_resize_node, figma_set_fills etc. 4) Re-screenshot and verify fix. 5) Iterate max 3 times — if still wrong, log the issue and try different approach. RULE: ALWAYS screenshot after creating components or complex layouts. Visual bugs are common with programmatic creation. Use figma_capture_screenshot over figma_take_screenshot for immediate verification (capture uses plugin runtime, take uses REST API which may show stale state).

After building a component, verify: 1) CREATE INSTANCE: figma_instantiate_component — does it create correctly? 2) OVERRIDE TEXT: figma_set_instance_properties — do text properties work? 3) TOGGLE BOOLEANS: do show/hide properties hide correct layers? 4) SWAP INSTANCES: do instance swap properties offer correct options? 5) SWITCH VARIANTS: do variant changes work smoothly? 6) RESIZE: does component respond correctly to width/height changes? 7) DARK MODE: switch variable mode — do all colors update? 8) Screenshot at each step to verify visually.

When user shares a reference Figma file to learn from: 1) figma_get_design_system_summary — get overview of components and tokens. 2) figma_get_variables(format='summary') — understand token structure. 3) figma_get_styles — extract typography and effects. 4) figma_search_components — browse component library. 5) For specific components: figma_get_component(nodeId, enrich=true) — full structure + token coverage. 6) figma_get_component_for_development — implementation-ready specs. 7) Synthesize findings into new JSONL records capturing: naming patterns, token structure, component architecture decisions, variant strategies. 8) Append to relevant lore modules or create project-specific module.

Complete design system audit workflow: 1) figma_get_design_system_summary — get overview. 2) figma_get_variables(enrich=true) — check token structure, coverage, exports. 3) figma_get_styles — check style usage and organization. 4) Run audit scripts from 09-audit-optimization module: detached instances scan, unnamed layers scan, hardcoded values scan. 5) figma_search_components + figma_get_component(enrich=true) on key components — check token coverage and property completeness. 6) Calculate health score using au-017 formula. 7) Generate prioritized report using au-016 template. 8) Screenshot key problem areas for evidence. 9) Propose fix plan: critical items first, then warnings, then improvements.

t: Recipe: Batch Rename Layers in Component
d: Rename all default-named layers inside a component: figma_execute: function renameDefaults(node) { const defaults = /^(Rectangle|Ellipse|Frame|Group|Line|Text|Vector|Star|Polygon|Image)\s*\d*$/; if (defaults.test(node.name)) { if (node.type === 'TEXT') node.name = node.characters.slice(0,30) || 'Text Content'; else if (node.type === 'RECTANGLE' || node.type === 'ELLIPSE') node.name = node.fills?.length ? 'Background' : 'Shape'; else if (node.type === 'FRAME') node.name = node.layoutMode ? 'Container' : 'Wrapper'; else node.name = node.type.toLowerCase(); } if ('children' in node) node.children.forEach(c => renameDefaults(c)); } const comp = figma.getNodeById('TARGET_ID'); renameDefaults(comp); return {renamed: true}; Then manually refine names with figma_rename_node for specific layers that need semantic names like 'Icon Container', 'CTA Button', 'Hero Image'.

Layer naming convention enforcement: RULE: Every layer inside a component MUST have a descriptive name before publishing. Naming patterns by type: FRAME with auto-layout → describe purpose ('Header','Content Area','Action Bar','Footer'). TEXT → describe role ('Title','Subtitle','Body Text','Helper Text','Label','Price','Badge Label'). RECTANGLE/ELLIPSE → describe function ('Background','Divider','Avatar Mask','Icon Background','Overlay'). INSTANCE → keep component name or describe context ('Leading Icon','Trailing Action','Avatar'). GROUP → convert to Frame, then name by purpose. Use figma_rename_node(nodeId, newName) for individual renames. Use figma_execute with recursive script for bulk operations.

t: Recipe: Rename Layers During Component Creation
d: CRITICAL WORKFLOW RULE: When creating components programmatically, ALWAYS name every layer immediately during creation — never leave default names. In figma_execute: every figma.createFrame() should immediately be followed by frame.name = 'Descriptive Name'. Every figma.createText() → text.name = 'Label' or text.name = 'Description'. Every figma_create_child call should include the name property: {name:'Icon Container',width:24,height:24}. POST-CREATION: Run naming audit script (au-005) on the component. Fix any remaining defaults. This prevents design debt from accumulating at creation time rather than cleaning up later.
